summary: Initialized separated project state directory for CapSched-Linux before fetching upstream Linux.
Separated AI handoff/protocol and general planning into dedicated ai/ and plans/ directories.
Split capsched-state into a minimal state ledger and moved AI, design, decisions, analysis, models, validation, plans, implementation, and templates into top-level sibling directories.

Collapsed project-control artifacts into two top-level directories: capsched-ai for AI/state/decisions/design continuity and capsched-models for modeling, code analysis, validation, plans, and implementation reasoning.
Aligned next action dependencies so upstream Linux fetch is blocked by project-control Git initialization.
Marked project-control Git initialization as complete and made upstream Linux fetch the next ready action.
Initialized workspace root as the project-control Git repository and set the initial branch to main.
Moved project-control repository into capsched/ and removed linux/ from project-control ignore rules so the future Linux repository is not hidden.
Recorded upstream Linux clone from git.kernel.org torvalds/linux, branch capsched-linux-l0 at commit 4edcdefd4083ae04b1a5656f4be6cd83ae919ef4.
Created active upstream Linux analysis plan and initial source map; began analysis without selecting implementation patch points.
Recorded deep compatibility, scheduler, lifecycle, existing-control, async provenance, cluster-domain, and invariant-matrix analysis before choosing any Linux patch point.
Expanded analysis to policy front-ends, mutable kernel state, dangerous service-domain surfaces, and selected the Runnable Lease formal model as the next gate before Linux patches.
Mapped network/socket endpoint operations and io_uring registered resources to EndpointCap and async provenance requirements; added execution plan for the Runnable Lease model.
Mapped BPF programmable policy boundaries and Linux cpuset/sched-domain topology to CapSched policy, compatibility, and cluster-cell constraints.
Created and checked the first Runnable Lease TLA+ model; TLC completed the tiny finite model with no invariant errors.
Derived the candidate Linux L0 Runnable Lease implementation plan from the checked TLA+ model and upstream scheduler/lifecycle/topology source maps; no Linux patch points accepted yet.
Selected Slice 0A as the first Linux patch candidate: inert CONFIG_CAPSCHED build scaffolding with no task layout or scheduler behavior changes; added out-of-tree build validation plan.
Applied and committed Linux Slice 0A inert CONFIG_CAPSCHED scaffolding as 0b685979f27b3d42ee620ced5f707ee391a2a27f; static checks passed, build validation is pending because flex is missing.

Started Slice 0A build validation under systemd user service capsched-linux-n010-build.service using rootless local flex, bison, and libelf files; fixed runner worktree detection and repo-local Git identity warnings.

Slice 0A build validation passed: upstream baseline, CONFIG_CAPSCHED=n, and CONFIG_CAPSCHED=y vmlinux builds completed; disabled config produced no capsched object and enabled config produced kernel/sched/capsched.o.
Created and checked the Endpoint Async Provenance TLA+ model; TLC completed the tiny finite model with no invariant errors, reinforcing No FrozenEndpointUse, no async endpoint execution.

Mapped Endpoint Async Provenance back to Linux io_uring, workqueue, task_work, socket, and LSM attachment points; identified io_kiocb/io_rsrc_node carriers, wrapper requirement for generic async work, and the socket sendmmsg nosec LSM-bypass hazard for CapSched endpoint checks.

Created and checked the Broker BudgetTicket TLA+ model; TLC completed the tiny finite model with no invariant errors, reinforcing that service authority alone cannot execute caller work without a live caller-reserved ticket and frozen endpoint authority.

Created and checked the Domain Monitor Activation TLA+ model without weakening the hostile Linux shadow-tag assumption; TLC completed primary and second runs with no invariant errors, reinforcing that Linux-visible DomainTag state is not execution authority without monitor-owned RunToken activation.
Added Cluster Lease Compilation full integration model and moved the long ClusterLease TLC run to a systemd user service without weakening the model; full pass/fail is pending service completion.

Added the Slice 0B readiness gate, integrating checked RunnableLease, EndpointAsync, BrokerBudget, DomainMonitor, and running ClusterLease model pressure before any type-only Linux authority scaffolding patch.

Mapped VFIO, iommufd, IOMMU DMA ownership, interrupt isolation, and representative NIC/NVMe queues to CapSched QueueLease requirements; recorded that VFIO/iommufd is compatibility substrate while production authority must be monitor-owned QueueTag, MemoryView/IOMMU map, IRQ route, epoch, and rate/budget.

Stopped the full ClusterLease systemd TLC run after state explosion without invariant errors and replaced it as proof root with decomposed ClusterShadowForgery and ClusterEpochRevoke models; both decomposed models passed TLC, preserving hostile mutable-claim and epoch-revoke assumptions while keeping TLC as auxiliary evidence rather than project objective.

Updated the Slice 0B readiness gate after decomposed cluster authority validation: Slice 0B is no longer blocked on full ClusterLease TLC completion, but remains limited to inert type scaffolding with opaque cluster placeholders, no hot struct fields, no behavior changes, no user ABI, and no Linux-only security claims.

Mapped upstream Linux struct page, folio, mm_struct, memcg, SLUB/slab, page allocator, and page-cache structures to CapSched Domain memory authority boundaries; recorded that Linux metadata is useful substrate but production memory authority requires monitor-owned PageOwner and MemoryView mappings.

Added the MemoryOwnership formal model set. The broad integration model was stopped after growth without invariant errors, then split into PageOwnerMemoryView, SlabObjGen, and MemoryWorkProvenance; all three decomposed models passed TLC and support monitor-owned PageOwner/MemoryView, slab generation, and memory work provenance invariants.

Added and checked the DirectMapTLB model. The first TLC run found a stale TLB-on-Domain-switch counterexample; after requiring Domain activation to flush or retag translations and strengthening shadow direct-map non-authority, TLC completed with 8224001 generated states and 386784 distinct states with no invariant errors.

Added and checked the PageCacheOverlay model. The first TLC run found a stale overlay commit counterexample; after requiring base-level commit serialization, TLC completed with 7370677 generated states and 524808 distinct states with no invariant errors.

Added and checked the QueueLease model. Queue submit, DMA mapping, IRQ delivery, epoch, and budget are modeled as one lease boundary; after strengthening IRQ aliasing, two TLC runs each explored 97882849 generated states and 6465312 distinct states with no invariant errors.

Added a protection-claim evidence map and assurance-driven achievement plan. The next gate is now Slice 0B inert type-only scaffolding plus a parallel assurance-case subclaim tree; behavior-changing scheduler hooks remain blocked.

Applied and committed Linux Slice 0B type-only authority scaffolding as 7cf0b1e415bcead8a2079c8be94a9d41aad7d462; build validation passed for CONFIG_CAPSCHED=n and CONFIG_CAPSCHED=y with no behavior-changing hooks or hot-struct attachment.

Created the assurance-case foundation for TOP-001 hypervisor-grade Domain separation, including a human-readable subclaim tree and machine-readable claims.json mapping activation, execution, budget, endpoint, async, memory, TLB, page-cache, device, revoke, cluster, compatibility, TCB, side-channel, and evaluation claims to evidence, gaps, counterexamples, forbidden L0 claims, and gates.

Selected the narrower wakeup/enqueue/runnable-state source coverage gate before Slice 0C trace code. Added analysis/0019 showing that activate_task alone is insufficient; future trace-only planning must cover try_to_wake_up self and normal paths, ttwu_runnable, delayed fair requeue, remote wakelist drain, wake_up_new_task, move_queued_task, affinity changes, pick/core scheduling, __schedule, sched_class lock contracts, and sched_ext custody paths.

Added the Slice 0C trace-only observation gate. The gate is tied to EXEC-001, COMPAT-001, and assurance gate G2, but recommends no Linux patch yet: first prepare a no-code trace run plan using existing scheduler tracepoints and dynamic ftrace. Any later patch must be CONFIG_CAPSCHED-gated internal observation only with no user ABI, no public tracepoint ABI, no task_struct authority fields, and no behavior changes.

Prepared the Slice 0C no-code trace validation plan and runner. The runner uses existing scheduler tracepoints and dynamic ftrace, saves and restores tracefs settings, records available and missing targets, captures trace output, and does not patch Linux. It has not been executed.

Checked Slice 0C trace execution readiness. The current session runs as uid 1000 user nia, tracefs is not writable, and the running kernel is Ubuntu 6.17.0-35-generic rather than a recorded CapSched worktree boot. The no-code trace runner was not executed.

Added Slice 0C trace analysis and workload planning. The analyzer classifies future trace output as observed, ambiguous, not_observed, or not_inferable, and the workload plan records default fork/exec, ping-pong, cross-CPU wake, affinity migration, scheduler pressure, and core/sched_ext probe categories. The trace runner was also adjusted to optionally run workloads as the original sudo user via CAPSCHED_TRACE_RUN_AS or SUDO_USER.

Added a userspace-only Slice 0C synthetic scheduler workload helper with forkexec, futex, affinity, pressure, and all modes. The helper compiled with gcc and passed small non-trace smoke tests for forkexec, futex, pressure, and affinity. It does not patch Linux or provide security evidence by itself.

Added the Slice 0C trace execution runbook with exact build, trace, analyze, and validation-result-template steps. The runbook keeps trace evidence bounded to observation and says the next real trace result should be recorded as validation/0020 rather than editing plans in place.

Added and executed the Slice 0C QEMU boot smoke harness. The successful run booted the CapSched worktree kernel with CONFIG_CAPSCHED=y and CONFIG_FUNCTION_TRACER=y, mounted tracefs, ran forkexec workload with WORKLOAD_RET 0, recorded qemu_status=0, and captured scheduler trace counts while preserving the observation-only claim boundary.
Updated the Slice 0C host trace runbook to avoid reusing validation IDs 0020 and 0021 after the QEMU boot validation plan and result were added.

Extended the Slice 0C QEMU runner to support workload-specific arguments and recorded broader QEMU workload results. futex cross, affinity, pressure, and all completed with WORKLOAD_RET 0 and qemu_status=0; persistent missing targets now point to ftrace/symbol eligibility or branch/argument visibility gaps rather than simple workload absence.

Analyzed QEMU vmlinux/System.map and scheduler source for persistent Slice 0C missing targets. ttwu_runnable, __ttwu_queue_wakelist, ttwu_queue, __pick_next_task, and pick_next_task are absent from the QEMU symbol table; __schedule exists but is notrace and kprobes-on-notrace is disabled. Recommended next step is a guest-side kprobe argument-capture experiment for visible targets such as enqueue_task.

Extended the Slice 0C QEMU runner with optional guest-side kprobes and recorded validation/0023. futex cross and affinity kprobe runs passed. enqueue_task flags were captured, including migration/rq-selected cases in clean reruns and one ENQUEUE_DELAYED case in an earlier successful affinity serial log; move_queued_task new_cpu was captured during affinity migration. The result remains observation-only and the next gate is a Slice 0C synthesis note, not enforcement.

Added Slice 0C observation synthesis, behavior tagging methodology, and subagent critical review. The review rejects the v1 behavior tag ledger for solver use and requires schema v2 with explicit claim scope, enforcement strength, trust root, attacker mutability, failure action, revocation scope, Linux scheduler ordering/locking/class/config context, separated cost/benefit vectors, and no distributed authority acquisition in scheduler hot paths.

Accepted ADR-0006: CapSched design is invariant-driven and tags are evidence/constraint indexes, not a design engine. Added analysis/0024, analysis/0025, and analysis/0026 to map Linux scheduler behavior into CapSched authority states and hook proof obligations before deriving schema v2 or selecting implementation hooks.

Derived schema v2 from the invariant-driven scheduler authority model and retagged Slice 0C behavior paths for gap analysis and hard reject. The new v2 ledger covers self-wake, already-runnable wake, normal wake admission, remote pending wake, new task wake, queued migration, generic enqueue, pick, switch, and explicit unmapped budget/exit placeholders; it remains not hook-selection eligible.

Added and checked the LinuxSchedulerAuthority formal model, plus source maps for tick/runtime budget and fork/clone/exec/exit identity propagation. TLC completed the tiny finite model with 126113 generated states, 17344 distinct states, depth 21, and no invariant errors. The work remains semantic evidence only; next steps are refinements before hook selection, not enforcement.

Added the TASK_WAKING failability boundary map and SchedulerAdmissionFailure model. The safe pre-TASK_WAKING rejection model passed TLC; unsafe delayed-freeze violated NoTaskWakingWithoutFrozenUse, and unsafe rollback violated NoLostWakeAfterCondition. The resulting rule is that fail-capable admission freeze must happen before TASK_WAKING; post-TASK_WAKING checks require nofail assertion, fail-closed stop, or separately proven rollback/quarantine.

Added the F1 admission-freeze data dependency map and F1AdmissionData model. The safe model passed TLC with 259 generated/distinct states; unsafe models produced expected counterexamples for slow lookup, allocation, monitor call, missing data, and missing placement under the pre-TASK_WAKING F1 boundary. The resulting rule is that F1 is validation/freeze, not authority discovery; required data must already be local, stable, and preallocated/prepared before p->pi_lock hot-path freeze.

Added the block/wait/register authority preparation source map and WakeAuthorityPreparation model. The safe model passed TLC with 18 generated states and 17 distinct states; unsafe models produced expected counterexamples for wake_q without prepared authority, F1 lazy discovery, and execution after revoke-between-wake_q-and-F1. The resulting rule is that generic wake paths must not be authority discovery interfaces; authority must be prepared in task-local, waiter-specific, endpoint-specific, work item, or service-domain state before wake_q_add(), wake_up_q(), or F1.

Added the task-local resumable-run lifecycle source map and TaskLocalRunState model. The safe model passed TLC with 41 generated states and 22 distinct states; unsafe models produced expected counterexamples for fork-copy authority inheritance, wake_up_new_task without prepared state, execution after revoke, and dead task authority retention. The resulting rule is that ordinary task-local run state must be reset after dup_task_struct, prepared before initial wake, frozen before TASK_WAKING, invalidated on revoke, and cleared before death/free.

Added the workqueue/kthread_work BudgetTicket carrier source map and WorkqueueCarrier model. The safe model passed TLC with 12 generated states and 8 distinct states; unsafe models produced expected counterexamples for carrierless queueing, ambient worker authority, execution after caller revoke, pending carrier overwrite, and completed work retaining authority refs. The resulting rule is that Domain-derived async worker execution needs a prepared typed carrier with FrozenEndpointUse and BudgetTicket before queue_work()/kthread_queue_work(); deep internal async redesign is allowed only if work classes remain typed instead of ambient.

Added the shared futex endpoint authority source map and SharedFutexEndpoint model. The safe model passed TLC with 16 generated states and 10 distinct states; unsafe models produced expected counterexamples for missing FutexWaitCap, missing FutexWakeCap, wake directly running a task, source-only requeue, endpoint use after revoke, and capability failure after queueing without rollback. The resulting rule is that shared/cross-Domain futexes are typed synchronization endpoints: wake authorizes signaling only, while target execution still requires task-local resumable-run freeze.

Added the PI/RT/ww_mutex priority donation authority source map and PriorityDonationAuthority model. The safe model passed TLC with 18 generated states and 13 distinct states; unsafe models produced expected counterexamples for no blocked dependency, missing donation cap, missing cross-Domain endpoint authority, boosted owner running without RunCap/FrozenRunUse, boosted owner running without budget, donation after release, wound/wait as thread control, and proxy-chain cycle. The resulting rule is that priority donation is dependency-derived ordering authority, not RunCap, SchedControlCap, ThreadControlCap, or free CPU budget.

Added the placement refresh authority source map and PlacementRefreshAuthority model. The safe model passed TLC with 13 generated states and 10 distinct states; unsafe models produced expected counterexamples for stale run, selected CPU outside frozen envelope, queued movement outside frozen envelope, fallback expansion creating authority, inactive CPU run, and migration-pending run. The resulting rule is that selected CPU is a hint, p->cpus_ptr is mutable Linux placement input, and FrozenRunUse.allowed_cpus plus fresh PlacementEpoch is the CapSched placement authority envelope.

Added the same-Domain monitor fast-path freshness source map and SameDomainFastPath model. The safe model passed TLC with 25 generated states and 13 distinct states; unsafe models produced expected counterexamples for stale epoch fast path, stale MemoryView run, run without root/SchedContext budget, NO_HZ without monitor timer, revoke while running, and selected budget staleness. The resulting rule is that same-Domain comparison is not no-check; monitor transition may be skipped only with local freshness proof, and capped NO_HZ execution requires monitor-owned or unsuppressible budget timer coverage.

Added the root/SchedContext/class-runtime budget split source map and BudgetSplitOverrun model. The safe model passed TLC with 16 generated states and 14 distinct states; unsafe models produced expected counterexamples for class runtime as root authority, no MonitorRootBudget, no SchedContextBudget, NO_HZ without monitor timer, hrtick floor overrun treated as exact cap, remote NO_HZ tick as root enforcer, and replenishment without epoch refresh. The resulting rule is that existing Linux class runtime may narrow execution but must never expand CapSched authority or replace monitor-owned root budget enforcement.

Added the class-specific selected-state source map and ClassSelectedState model. The safe model passed TLC with 47 generated states and 27 distinct states; unsafe models produced expected counterexamples for running without FrozenRunUse, no class revalidation after pick, stale core cached pick, deadline-server borrow without ticket, sched_ext slice as authority, proxy execution without ticket, and class state mutation after revalidation. The resulting rule is that class pick is selection, not authority; final execution needs class-specific revalidation after core/server/proxy/SCX/class state has settled.

Added the wider endpoint capability source map and EndpointAuthority model. The safe model passed TLC with 57 generated states and 43 distinct states; unsafe models produced expected counterexamples for fd lookup as authority, open basis as all-operation authority, socket nosec bypass, transfer without derivation, ambient worker endpoint execution, revoked queued/registered use, mmap without MmapCap, and ioctl without typed command authority. The resulting rule is that object reachability is not endpoint authority: endpoint effects require operation-specific FrozenEndpointUse, transfers require derived receiver authority, and async workers need caller frozen authority plus BudgetTicket rather than ambient worker authority.

Added the exec generation and inherited endpoint semantics source map and ExecGeneration model. The safe model passed TLC with 11 generated states and 11 distinct states; unsafe models produced expected counterexamples for Domain change without token, run after exec without continuation, old FrozenEndpointUse after exec, surviving fd without derivation, close-on-exec leak, credential/LSM amplification, execfd without derivation, old async use after exec, old mmap survival, and AT_EXECVE_CHECK mutation. The resulting rule is that ordinary exec does not change CapSched Domain, but successful exec is a new ProgramGeneration boundary and all surviving endpoint reachability must be derived or attenuated before post-exec effects.

Added the post-exec resource inheritance class map and PostExecResource model. The safe model passed TLC with 17 generated states and 17 distinct states; unsafe models produced expected counterexamples for generic fd authority, close-on-exec leak, regular file operation without mask, socket ambient state, generic anon fd policy, epoll old readiness, eventfd signal leak, timerfd old authority, io_uring registered resource leak, and execfd ambient inheritance. The resulting rule is that post-exec fd reachability is not endpoint authority; each surviving resource class requires class-specific derivation or attenuation before endpoint effects.

Added the post-exec resource trace-only coverage map and validation plan. The map separates raw syscall fd-surface observation, sched exec boundary tracepoints, io_uring tracepoints, workqueue tracepoints, socket tracepoints, and kprobe-visible internal anchors from semantic endpoint authority derivation. The resulting rule is that trace-only evidence can guide hook visibility and blind-spot analysis, but cannot prove post-exec endpoint protection; the next step is an observation-only QEMU workload/runner extension that classifies each resource class as observed, partially_observed, not_observed, or not_trace_provable.

Added and executed the post-exec resource QEMU trace runner and workload. Final run 20260627T100204Z reused the existing CapSched QEMU bzImage, exited with qemu_status=0 and workload_ret=0, and classified CLOEXEC, regular file, O_PATH, socket, anonfd creation, and timerfd as observed; eventfd, epoll, and io_uring as partially observed; and execfd as not observed. The result remains observation-only and identifies the next gaps: eventfd kernel signal provenance, epoll watched-endpoint delivery, io_uring fixed-file consumption, and execfd handoff.

Added the workqueue internal redesign boundary note. The note accepts deep internal async redesign as a likely production requirement, but rejects internal-only trust or ambient worker authority. It records the two decisive Linux facts: queue_work() coalesces already-pending work_struct instances, so caller BudgetTickets cannot be overwritten safely; and worker->current_func(work) / work->func(work) executes in worker or kthread context, so caller authority is not naturally preserved. The immediate rule remains that Domain-derived async work needs typed carriers before leaving caller context, while kernel-internal work must stay explicitly service/kernel classified until proven otherwise.

Added the workqueue origin taxonomy and observation plan. The taxonomy separates PerInvocation, ExplicitMerge, ServiceOnly, KernelException, InterruptDeferred, ReclaimRescue, TaskLocal, and unknown_or_mixed paths before any generic workqueue enforcement hook. It includes a machine-readable JSON taxonomy and records that workqueue/kthread tracepoints expose work pointer, callback, queue name, and execution events, but queue-site stack or source correlation plus endpoint-effect and merge semantics are required before making authority claims. N-063 is completed; next work is a taxonomy-driven source inventory or QEMU observation runner.

Added and executed the workqueue origin source inventory runner. Final run 20260627T102126Z emitted workqueue-origin-classification.tsv with 10 source-inferred seed classifications and workqueue-origin-gaps.tsv with 49 gap rows. The largest unknown groups are drivers/net, drivers/gpu, drivers/scsi, drivers/usb, sound/soc, drivers/media, drivers/power, and drivers/infiniband. The result is observation-only and explicitly does not justify generic workqueue enforcement.

Added and executed the dedicated drivers/net workqueue origin source inventory runner. Final run 20260627T102701Z emitted 1440 callsite rows, 164 family/subfamily rows, 10 API rows, 40 hotspot rows, and 18 gap rows. The largest groups are wireless/intel, ethernet/intel, ethernet/mellanox, ethernet/marvell, and wireless/ath. The result decomposes drivers/net from one bulk unknown into candidate taxonomy projections, but remains observation-only and does not justify a net driver or generic workqueue enforcement hook.

Added the representative usbnet workqueue source map and machine-readable source-map JSON. The map shows bh_work is INIT_WORK-bound to usbnet_bh_work, drains dev->done, processes rx/tx completion cleanup, refills RX, wakes TX, and merges through dev->done plus the work pending bit; kevent is INIT_WORK-bound to usbnet_deferred_kevent, merges event bits in dev->flags, and performs USB/network control-plane recovery. The resulting rule is that caller-derived Network EndpointCap or QueueLease checks belong at submit/request boundaries, while merged completion/control work must use service authority or aggregate QueueLease settlement, never a single overwritten caller BudgetTicket on shared work_struct callbacks.

Added the representative e1000e QueueLease source map and machine-readable source-map JSON. The map shows the Ethernet data plane is ring/IRQ/NAPI based: .ndo_start_xmit enters e1000_xmit_frame, maps SKBs for DMA, publishes descriptors, and writes the TX tail doorbell; completion is handled by IRQ/NAPI through e1000_clean_tx_irq, clean_rx, and e1000e_poll. e1000e workqueue users are reset/watchdog/PHY/diagnostic/timestamp service paths, not generic per-caller carriers. The resulting rule is that NIC authority should be modeled as QueueLease submit, in-flight descriptor ledger, IRQ/NAPI ownership, DMA/IOMMU ownership, and completion settlement rather than workqueue callback authority.

Added and checked the Aggregate QueueLease Settlement model. The safe configuration passed TLC with 16 generated states, 11 distinct states, and depth 7. Unsafe configurations produced expected counterexamples for tail doorbell without live QueueLease, submit without budget, DMA without IOMMU/ledger, completion without ledger, completion without service budget, delivery after revoke, pending ledger overwrite, ambient completion authority, and foreign completion. The resulting rule is that submit authority belongs at QueueLease/DMA/doorbell boundaries while merged completion work performs aggregate settlement only; no generic workqueue enforcement or single mutable caller ticket on a shared callback object follows from this.

Added the observation-only Linux queue/descriptor ledger tag plan and schema. The plan records that existing netdev, NAPI, IRQ, SKB, IOMMU, and DMA tracepoints provide useful outer visibility, but descriptor publish, tail doorbell, submit-ledger correlation, completion settlement, and revoke/drop outcomes require driver-specific observation or later trace-only CapSched tags. It answers the internal-redesign question by accepting deep Linux async/device redesign as a typed substrate while rejecting internal-only trust: production authority still needs proof-visible ledgers/carriers and monitor-owned QueueTag, IOMMU, epoch, and budget roots. N-070 is completed; N-071 is the observation-only runner or static/probe readiness checker.

Added and executed the queue/descriptor ledger static readiness checker. Final run 20260627T110900Z found 14 existing netdev/NAPI/IRQ/SKB/IOMMU/DMA tracepoint rows with zero missing rows and 25 e1000e source anchors with zero missing rows. It produced 12 event readiness rows and 8 semantic gaps; every readiness row carries observation_only=true, authority_claim=false, and monitor_verified=false. High-severity gaps remain for SubmitLedger id, DMA-to-submit correlation, descriptor publication, tail doorbell, completion ledger settlement, revoke semantics, and authority-root. The authority-root gap is decisive: all observed state is Linux-mutable and monitor_verified=false, so this output is only hook-placement and modeling evidence, not protection evidence. N-071 is completed; N-069 remains the next source-analysis target for a modern multi-queue NIC path.

Added the Intel ice modern NIC QueueLease source map and machine-readable source-map JSON. The map covers VSI/ring/q_vector/IRQ/NAPI binding, SKB TX DMA/descriptor/doorbell path, XDP frame and XDP_TX page-pool paths, AF_XDP zero-copy descriptor batches, TX/RX completion settlement, driver tracepoints, devlink rate/scheduling controls, SR-IOV/SF/representor surfaces, and service/reset/PTP/DPLL/eswitch work. It answers the internal-reimplementation question by accepting deep internal async/device redesign as a typed substrate while rejecting internal-only trust: caller-derived authority must appear as proof-visible carriers or ledgers before queue_work coalescing, worker execution, queue submit, or completion settlement can lose caller identity. N-069 is completed; N-072 and N-073 are now the next modern NIC model/readiness tasks.

Added and checked the Modern NIC QueueLease model. The safe configuration passed TLC with 1474 generated states, 701 distinct states, and depth 12. Unsafe configurations produced expected counterexamples for submit without QueueBind, submit without budget, SKB submit without IOMMU proof, XDP submit using an SKB ledger/capability, AF_XDP zero-copy submit without XSK/UMEM ownership, representor forwarding without derived lower queue authority, devlink queue-control through RunCap, service/reset/PTP/DPLL work charged to the last submitter, completion by ambient worker/service authority, and delivery after revoke. N-072 is completed; N-073 remains the next observation-only ice readiness task.

Added and executed the ice modern NIC observation-only static readiness checker. Final run 20260627T113618Z emitted 19 tracepoint rows with zero missing rows, 40 source anchor rows with zero missing rows, 11 class readiness rows, and 12 high-severity semantic gaps. Every readiness row carries observation_only=true, authority_claim=false, and monitor_verified=false. The result confirms useful observability for QueueBind, SKB submit, XDP, AF_XDP, DescriptorLedger, CompletionSettlement, QueueControl, RepresentorForward, ServiceWork, and RevokeSemantics, but records that none is protection evidence because monitor QueueTag, Domain epoch, typed ledger ids, QueueControlCap, lower QueueLease derivation, service BudgetTicket, and revoke epoch/quarantine outcome are absent. N-073 is completed; N-074 and N-075 are next modeling targets.

Added and checked the XDP and AF_XDP Memory Ownership model. The safe configuration passed TLC with 19 generated states, 13 distinct states, and depth 6. Unsafe configurations produced expected counterexamples for XDP_TX submit without page-pool ownership, AF_XDP submit without XSK/UMEM ownership, DMA submit without live MemoryView, ambient AF_XDP descriptor use without freeze, cross-Domain DMA, completion without typed ledger, double return of packet memory, return after revoke, and submit without budget. DEVICE-009 is added: XDP_TX page-pool reuse and AF_XDP zero-copy submit require explicit memory ownership authority and cannot borrow queue reachability, SKB authority, generic XDP authority, or ambient driver state. N-074 is completed; N-075 remains the next modern NIC modeling target.

Added and checked the QueueControl and RepresentorForward model. The safe configuration passed TLC with 7 generated states, 7 distinct states, and depth 3. Unsafe configurations produced expected counterexamples for devlink queue-control through RunCap, devlink queue-control through plain netdev reachability, representor forwarding without RepresentorForwardCap, representor forwarding without lower QueueLease, representor forwarding with stale lower queue epoch, representor forwarding by plain netdev reachability, representor forwarding without service budget, queue control after revoke, and representor forwarding after revoke. DEVICE-010 is added: devlink/rate/scheduler/VF/SF/representor lifecycle authority is QueueControl authority, and representor transmit requires RepresentorForward authority plus a live lower QueueLease. N-075 is completed; N-076 is the next assurance consolidation step.

Consolidated modern NIC QueueLease evidence into assurance/0002 and a machine-readable subclaim map. DEV-001 now has DEV-NIC-001 through DEV-NIC-010 covering QueueBind, typed submit classes, DescriptorLedger, DMA packet memory ownership, CompletionSettlement, QueueControl, RepresentorForward, ServiceWork/async provenance, RevokeSemantics, and Linux substrate compatibility. The gate result is model-supported authority-class separation, source-observed Intel ice anchors, observation-only readiness, no production protection evidence, and no implementation approval. N-076 is completed; N-077 is the next revoke/drain/quarantine modeling task.

Added and checked the Modern NIC Queue Revoke model. Safe TLC passed with 7 generated states, 7 distinct states, and depth 5. Unsafe configs produced expected counterexamples for submit after revoke, completion delivery after revoke, QueueControl after revoke, RepresentorForward after revoke, service work after revoke, ledger clear before DMA drain, queue reassignment before drain/quarantine, queue reassignment without IOMMU/IRQ invalidation, and quarantined delivery. N-077 is completed; N-078 is the next ice source-map task.

Mapped formal/0031 modern NIC revoke obligations back to Intel ice source. Useful anchors include ice_down, IRQ masking/synchronize paths, NAPI disable, ring stop and clean, XSK clean, queue-pair disable/enable, reset preparation, service task stop, representor queue stop, and devlink reload down/up. The map preserves hard gaps for QueueTag/epoch, typed ledgers, monitor IOMMU/MemoryView invalidation, stale XSK/page-pool quarantine, VF IRQ ownership exception, lower QueueLease representor revoke, service carrier authority, and old/new epoch reassignment proof. N-078 is completed; N-079 is the next observation-only checker design task.

Added and executed the ice revoke observation-only static readiness checker.
